{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Software Engineer - PHP",
  "description": "Crown Interactive delivers innovative, cost-effective, service aggregation solutions to the Telecom, Media and Technology-based sectors. Starting in 2005, this London-based business has gained ground-breaking expertise integrating business systems with evolving information technology.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Software&nbsp;&nbsp;Engineer - PHP&nbsp;

Job Location: Lagos

Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical discipline.
	Minimum of 6 years of professional experience as a PHP Software Engineer.
	Experience developing enterprise applications.
	Experience within the power/energy sector is an added advantage.
	Knowledge of Java is an added advantage.


Required Skills &amp; Technical Competencies:


	Strong proficiency in PHP 7+ and Object-Oriented Programming (OOP).
	Hands-on experience with Yii1, Yii2, and Laravel.
	Strong knowledge of MySQL and database design.
	Experience working in Linux environments.
	Experience with MVC frameworks such as Laravel, Symfony, or CodeIgniter.
	Solid understanding of SQL optimization and database performance.
	Working knowledge of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript (experience with Vue.js, React, or Angular is an advantage).
	Experience using Git-based version control systems (GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket).
	Experience building and integrating RESTful APIs (SOAP knowledge is an added advantage).
	Understanding of secure coding practices and application performance optimization.
	Experience building workflow management applications and enterprise software platforms.
	Familiarity with SaaS application deployment.
	Exposure to cloud platforms such as AWS, Microsoft Azure, or Google Cloud is an advantage.


Core Competencies:


	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to lead technical teams and mentor junior engineers.
	Excellent communication and collaboration skills.
	Ability to work independently and within cross-functional teams.
	Experience working in Agile development environments is an advantage.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: careers@crowninteractive.com or recruitment@cicod.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.&nbsp;",

Software Engineer - PHP Job at Crown Interactive




        
    

        
        Crown Interactive delivers innovative, cost-effective, service aggregation solutions to the Telecom, Media and Technology-based sectors. Starting in 2005, this London-based business has gained ground-breaking expertise integrating business systems with evolving information technology.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Software  Engineer - PHP 

Job Location: Lagos

Qualifications & Experience
     

            
    




	Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical discipline.
	Minimum of 6 years of professional experience as a PHP Software Engineer.
	Experience developing enterprise applications.
	Experience within the power/energy sector is an added advantage.
	Knowledge of Java is an added advantage.


Required Skills & Technical Competencies:


	Strong proficiency in PHP 7+ and Object-Oriented Programming (OOP).
	Hands-on experience with Yii1, Yii2, and Laravel.
	Strong knowledge of MySQL and database design.
	Experience working in Linux environments.
	Experience with MVC frameworks such as Laravel, Symfony, or CodeIgniter.
	Solid understanding of SQL optimization and database performance.
	Working knowledge of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript (experience with Vue.js, React, or Angular is an advantage).
	Experience using Git-based version control systems (GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket).
	Experience building and integrating RESTful APIs (SOAP knowledge is an added advantage).
	Understanding of secure coding practices and application performance optimization.
	Experience building workflow management applications and enterprise software platforms.
	Familiarity with SaaS application deployment.
	Exposure to cloud platforms such as AWS, Microsoft Azure, or Google Cloud is an advantage.


Core Competencies:


	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to lead technical teams and mentor junior engineers.
	Excellent communication and collaboration skills.
	Ability to work independently and within cross-functional teams.
	Experience working in Agile development environments is an advantage.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] or [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.     

    

        Click here to Apply

	Must reside along stadium road, Taiwo unity, Irewolode, Asa-Dam and Oko-erin axis.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: Oribaseskincare@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Sales Representative / Attendant Job at Oribase Skincare




        
    

        
        Oribase Skincare (also known as Oribase Skincare & Med Aesthetic Spa) is a Nigerian skincare and wellness brand that specializes in clinically effective, science-baked skincare products and spa treatments. They offer specialized services like facials, IV therapy, and traditional spa treatments, alongside retailing their own formulated items like the Oribase Hydrating Face Cream

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Representative / Attendant

Job Location: Ilorin, Kwara 

Requirements
     

            
    




	Must have 1 - 2 years of experience.
	With no experience, must be eager to learn on the job
	Must reside along stadium road, Taiwo unity, Irewolode, Asa-Dam and Oko-erin axis.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: For more enquiries, contact: 08168231261, 09016188664.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Project Manager - Salesforce CRM Implementation",
  "description": "Renmoney - We are a passionate team determined to challenge the status quo and make financial inclusion count for the millions of under-banked individuals and small business owners in Nigeria. We provide loans, savings, and fixed deposit solutions to our customers. Our vision is to be the most convenient lending company, delivering outstanding service experiences.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Project Manager - Salesforce CRM Implementation

Job Location: Lagos

The Position


	We are seeking an experienced and commercially astute Project Manager to lead the end-to-end implementation of Salesforce CRM across the business.
	This role will drive the full lifecycle of the Salesforce deployment, including solution design, stakeholder alignment, system integration, implementation, adoption, and optimization. A key focus will be ensuring seamless integration between Salesforce, Rencore, and Mambu to support customer management, loan origination, deposit onboarding, and card product workflows.
	As the Salesforce Champion, you will work closely with business and technology teams to deliver a platform that drives operational efficiency, customer experience, and business growth.


Responsibilities
Salesforce Project Delivery:


	Lead full lifecycle Salesforce implementations and enhancements&mdash;from requirements gathering and solution design through configuration, integration, testing, data migration, training, go-live, and post-go-live optimization.
	Develop and manage detailed project plans, timelines, budgets, resource plans, and risk registers using Agile, Waterfall, or hybrid methodologies.
	Serve as the Salesforce Implementation Champion by building executive sponsorship, driving cross-functional alignment, and promoting user adoption across departments.
	Work closely with Salesforce architects, developers, administrators, and external implementation partners to deliver high-quality solutions.
	Facilitate workshops, process mapping, gap analysis, and requirements sessions to translate business needs into effective Salesforce configurations and automations.
	Lead change management, communication plans, and training programs to maximize user adoption and minimize resistance.
	Manage project budgets, vendor relationships, and resource allocation to deliver projects on time and within budget.
	Deliver the Salesforce CRM implementation aligned to business goals across the Loans, Deposits, and Cards business lines.


Project Planning &amp; Governance:


	Develop and maintain a comprehensive project plan covering scope, milestones, resource allocation, risk management, and budget tracking.
	Establish governance structures including steering committee meetings, working groups, and cross-functional status reviews.
	Monitor and report project health metrics including RAG status, issue logs, dependency maps, and change control.
	Apply structured project management methodologies appropriate to the implementation context.


Systems Integration &amp; Technical Oversight:


	Lead the design and delivery of the Salesforce &rarr; Rencore &rarr; Mambu integration model.
	Define data flow specifications, field mapping, and API integration requirements with architects and integration teams.
	Define and validate condition-based triggers governing when customer information is propagated into Mambu.
	Oversee data migration, system integrations, and quality assurance activities.
	Coordinate User Acceptance Testing (UAT), System Integration Testing (SIT), and regression testing cycles.


Product Workflow Delivery:


	Oversee Salesforce workflow design and configuration for Loans, Deposits, and Cards product lines.
	Ensure workflows align with regulatory, compliance, and operational requirements.
	Support:
	
		Loans: Lead capture, credit application intake, document collection stages, underwriting handoff, and disbursement triggers.
		Deposits: Account opening workflows, customer onboarding journeys, compliance documentation, and activation conditions.
		Cards: Card application management, eligibility checks, approval workflows, and fulfilment integration.
	
	


Customer &amp; Lead Data Management:


	Define data governance standards for customers and lead information managed within Salesforce, including mandatory fields, data quality rules, and lifecycle stages.
	Establish validation frameworks that determine when a lead record matures into a customer profile eligible for downstream system population.
	Ensure the integrity and completeness of data migrated or created in Salesforce, with traceability into Rencore and Mambu.


Stakeholder &amp; Change Management:


	Serve as the primary point of accountability for all Salesforce implementation workstreams with internal and external stakeholders.
	Manage relationships with implementation partners, Rencore technical teams, and Mambu integration support.
	Lead organisational readiness activities, including training programmes, process documentation, and adoption tracking.
	Proactively manage stakeholder expectations and escalate risks or blockers through appropriate governance channels.


Compliance &amp; Regulatory Alignment:


	Ensure the implementation adheres to NDPR and applicable regulatory requirements.
	Collaborate with Compliance and Legal teams to ensure AML, KYC, and credit risk requirements are embedded within workflows.
	Ensure audit trails are built into Salesforce configurations to support regulatory reporting and audit requirements.


Requirements
Education &amp; Experience:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Business Administration, or a related field; Master&rsquo;s degree is an advantage.
	Minimum of 7 years of project management experience, with at least 3 years managing CRM or enterprise software implementations.
	Proven track record delivering Salesforce implementations in complex, multi-stakeholder environments.
	Demonstrated experience managing system integration projects involving multiple platforms, APIs, and data pipelines.
	Experience in financial services or fintech, with knowledge of core banking concepts, loan origination, deposit management, or card processing.
	PMP, PRINCE2, SAFe, or equivalent project management certification required.


Technical Expertise:


	Strong knowledge of Salesforce platforms including Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, or Financial Services Cloud.
	Experience managing integration architecture, APIs, data migration, and data quality initiatives.
	Familiarity with Mambu or similar cloud-native core banking platforms.
	Proficiency with project management tools such as JIRA, MS Project, Asana, and Confluence.


Preferred Qualifications:


	Salesforce Administrator or Salesforce Project Management certification.
	Experience working in regulated financial services environments.
	Knowledge of CBN regulatory frameworks.
	Experience supporting large-scale digital transformation programmes.


This Job Is Perfect For You If You:


	Have successfully delivered enterprise CRM implementations from planning through deployment.
	Can balance business priorities, technical requirements, and stakeholder expectations.
	Thrive in complex, cross-functional environments.
	Have strong systems thinking and integration experience.
	Are highly organized, commercially minded, and delivery focused.

Project Manager - Salesforce CRM Implementation Job at Renmoney




        
    

        
        Renmoney - We are a passionate team determined to challenge the status quo and make financial inclusion count for the millions of under-banked individuals and small business owners in Nigeria. We provide loans, savings, and fixed deposit solutions to our customers. Our vision is to be the most convenient lending company, delivering outstanding service experiences.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Project Manager - Salesforce CRM Implementation

Job Location: Lagos

The Position
     

            
    




	We are seeking an experienced and commercially astute Project Manager to lead the end-to-end implementation of Salesforce CRM across the business.
	This role will drive the full lifecycle of the Salesforce deployment, including solution design, stakeholder alignment, system integration, implementation, adoption, and optimization. A key focus will be ensuring seamless integration between Salesforce, Rencore, and Mambu to support customer management, loan origination, deposit onboarding, and card product workflows.
	As the Salesforce Champion, you will work closely with business and technology teams to deliver a platform that drives operational efficiency, customer experience, and business growth.


Responsibilities
Salesforce Project Delivery:


	Lead full lifecycle Salesforce implementations and enhancements—from requirements gathering and solution design through configuration, integration, testing, data migration, training, go-live, and post-go-live optimization.
	Develop and manage detailed project plans, timelines, budgets, resource plans, and risk registers using Agile, Waterfall, or hybrid methodologies.
	Serve as the Salesforce Implementation Champion by building executive sponsorship, driving cross-functional alignment, and promoting user adoption across departments.
	Work closely with Salesforce architects, developers, administrators, and external implementation partners to deliver high-quality solutions.
	Facilitate workshops, process mapping, gap analysis, and requirements sessions to translate business needs into effective Salesforce configurations and automations.
	Lead change management, communication plans, and training programs to maximize user adoption and minimize resistance.
	Manage project budgets, vendor relationships, and resource allocation to deliver projects on time and within budget.
	Deliver the Salesforce CRM implementation aligned to business goals across the Loans, Deposits, and Cards business lines.


Project Planning & Governance:


	Develop and maintain a comprehensive project plan covering scope, milestones, resource allocation, risk management, and budget tracking.
	Establish governance structures including steering committee meetings, working groups, and cross-functional status reviews.
	Monitor and report project health metrics including RAG status, issue logs, dependency maps, and change control.
	Apply structured project management methodologies appropriate to the implementation context.


Systems Integration & Technical Oversight:


	Lead the design and delivery of the Salesforce → Rencore → Mambu integration model.
	Define data flow specifications, field mapping, and API integration requirements with architects and integration teams.
	Define and validate condition-based triggers governing when customer information is propagated into Mambu.
	Oversee data migration, system integrations, and quality assurance activities.
	Coordinate User Acceptance Testing (UAT), System Integration Testing (SIT), and regression testing cycles.


Product Workflow Delivery:


	Oversee Salesforce workflow design and configuration for Loans, Deposits, and Cards product lines.
	Ensure workflows align with regulatory, compliance, and operational requirements.
	Support:
	
		Loans: Lead capture, credit application intake, document collection stages, underwriting handoff, and disbursement triggers.
		Deposits: Account opening workflows, customer onboarding journeys, compliance documentation, and activation conditions.
		Cards: Card application management, eligibility checks, approval workflows, and fulfilment integration.
	
	


Customer & Lead Data Management:


	Define data governance standards for customers and lead information managed within Salesforce, including mandatory fields, data quality rules, and lifecycle stages.
	Establish validation frameworks that determine when a lead record matures into a customer profile eligible for downstream system population.
	Ensure the integrity and completeness of data migrated or created in Salesforce, with traceability into Rencore and Mambu.


Stakeholder & Change Management:


	Serve as the primary point of accountability for all Salesforce implementation workstreams with internal and external stakeholders.
	Manage relationships with implementation partners, Rencore technical teams, and Mambu integration support.
	Lead organisational readiness activities, including training programmes, process documentation, and adoption tracking.
	Proactively manage stakeholder expectations and escalate risks or blockers through appropriate governance channels.


Compliance & Regulatory Alignment:


	Ensure the implementation adheres to NDPR and applicable regulatory requirements.
	Collaborate with Compliance and Legal teams to ensure AML, KYC, and credit risk requirements are embedded within workflows.
	Ensure audit trails are built into Salesforce configurations to support regulatory reporting and audit requirements.


Requirements
Education & Experience:


	Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Business Administration, or a related field; Master’s degree is an advantage.
	Minimum of 7 years of project management experience, with at least 3 years managing CRM or enterprise software implementations.
	Proven track record delivering Salesforce implementations in complex, multi-stakeholder environments.
	Demonstrated experience managing system integration projects involving multiple platforms, APIs, and data pipelines.
	Experience in financial services or fintech, with knowledge of core banking concepts, loan origination, deposit management, or card processing.
	PMP, PRINCE2, SAFe, or equivalent project management certification required.


Technical Expertise:


	Strong knowledge of Salesforce platforms including Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, or Financial Services Cloud.
	Experience managing integration architecture, APIs, data migration, and data quality initiatives.
	Familiarity with Mambu or similar cloud-native core banking platforms.
	Proficiency with project management tools such as JIRA, MS Project, Asana, and Confluence.


Preferred Qualifications:


	Salesforce Administrator or Salesforce Project Management certification.
	Experience working in regulated financial services environments.
	Knowledge of CBN regulatory frameworks.
	Experience supporting large-scale digital transformation programmes.


This Job Is Perfect For You If You:


	Have successfully delivered enterprise CRM implementations from planning through deployment.
	Can balance business priorities, technical requirements, and stakeholder expectations.
	Thrive in complex, cross-functional environments.
	Have strong systems thinking and integration experience.
	Are highly organized, commercially minded, and delivery focused.

	The successful candidate will support safe operations, conduct audits, manage incident reporting, and drive continuous improvement in HSSE performance.


Key Responsibilities


	Implement and monitor the company&#39;s HSSE policies, procedures, and management systems.
	Conduct workplace inspections, risk assessments, Job Hazard Analyses (JHAs), and safety audits.
	Ensure compliance with client HSSE requirements, statutory regulations, and industry standards.

	Monitor the use of personal protective equipment (PPE) and ensure compliance with safe&nbsp;&nbsp;work practices.
	Maintain HSSE records, reports, statistics, and performance KPIs.

	Liaise with regulatory agencies, clients, and external auditors during inspections and audits.
	Support the implementation and maintenance of ISO 45001, ISO 14001, and ISO 9001 management systems.
	Promote a positive safety culture throughout the organization.


Salary
N150,000 / month.

HSSE Officer Job at Career Breedge Consultancy Limited




        
    

        
        Career Breedge Consultancy, an HR solutions organization, is  recruiting to fill the position below: 

 Job Position: HSSE Officer

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The HSSE Officer will be responsible for implementing and maintaining the company's Health, Safety, Security, and Environmental (HSSE) management system, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements, client standards, and industry best practices.
	The successful candidate will support safe operations, conduct audits, manage incident reporting, and drive continuous improvement in HSSE performance.


Key Responsibilities


	Implement and monitor the company's HSSE policies, procedures, and management systems.
	Conduct workplace inspections, risk assessments, Job Hazard Analyses (JHAs), and safety audits.
	Ensure compliance with client HSSE requirements, statutory regulations, and industry standards.
	Coordinate safety inductions, toolbox talks, drills, and HSSE training programs.
	Investigate incidents, near misses, and unsafe conditions, and recommend corrective and preventive actions.
	Monitor the use of personal protective equipment (PPE) and ensure compliance with safe  work practices.
	Maintain HSSE records, reports, statistics, and performance KPIs.
	Participate in emergency response planning and emergency drills.
	Liaise with regulatory agencies, clients, and external auditors during inspections and audits.
	Support the implementation and maintenance of ISO 45001, ISO 14001, and ISO 9001 management systems.
	Promote a positive safety culture throughout the organization.


Salary
N150,000 / month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV and a cover letter detailing their relevant experience, qualifications, and achievements to: careerbreedgeconsults.com and copy [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Direct Sales Executive Job at ipNX Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        ipNX is one of Nigeria’s fastest growing Information and Communications Technology companies, serving a multitude of needs across enterprises, small businesses and residents with innovative, world-class services.

Our ability to identify, satisfy and exceed today’s market needs is a testament to over a decade of experience, our commitment, drive and passion realized through highly skilled and well seasoned professionals.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Direct Sales Executive

Job Location: Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time 

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Direct  sales executive (DSE) is responsible for driving sales increase of all ipNX retail products (FOS) within the territory he works in.
	She/he must recruit new subscribers through personal engagement and follow up.
	The DSE must be able to develop relationships with Estate managers, facility managers and any key stakeholder.
	DSE’s must be able to win back churned customers and re-build broken relationships.


Responsibilities


	The DSE must recruit 30 subscribers or more every month.
	Must be able to negotiate terms of an agreement to a satisfactory Level and close sales with minimal supervision.
	The DSE must maintain and grow a prospect list. This pipeline report must be submitted on a daily basis – 10 unique prospects everyday.
	Must carry out route mapping to identify potential and existing customers with given territory.
	Cold call potential customers in a bid to get a foot in the door
	Effectively act as brand ambassadors and first point of contact to potential customers
	All sales reports must be submitted every week.
	Must be able to maintain a good working relationship with Team Lead and other DSE’s.
	Must be polite at all times to customers and report any customer feedback during weekly meetings.
	Must be willing to work at least 3 weekends every month.
	Must be present at all sales training and weekly meetings.
	Must abide by the ipNX policies and standards when on ipNX premises and in dealing with customers.


Qualifications & Experience


	HND qualification in Engineering / Computer Science / Marketing / Sales / related disciplines
	At least 2 years of experience within the telecoms or FMCG space; knowledge of retail market, competitor behavior and strategy, including related product dynamics and product management.
	Understanding of communications Network, Experience in a b2b, B2C sales environment; field sales, consistent track record of success in achieving product effectiveness and customer experience targets.
	Demonstrate a high level of analytical thinking, gather and analyze information and provide viable insight about market trends and customer behavior.
	Demonstrate a high level of organization and quality with multitasks, efficiency & priority setting.
	Must demonstrate interpersonal capabilities and leadership.
	Exemplary communication skills – both face to face and over the telephone. Ability to communicate effectively at all levels of an organization and favorably project ipNX products & services
	Good Microsoft office suite competence.


Salary
N230,000 monthly.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply
